# Unofficial Meeting Notes
## Conroe Independent School District Board of Trustees
### Regular Meeting — March 22, 2022

> **Note:** These are **unofficial notes** based on an auto-generated transcript of the meeting. They may contain errors or omissions. Please consult official board minutes for the authoritative record.

---

## Call to Order
- Meeting called to order at 6:00 PM by President Hubert
- Quorum confirmed present
- Invocation led by David Williams; Pledge of Allegiance led by Scott Moore

---

## Awards & Recognition (Item 2A)

### Caney Creek High School — State Wrestling Champion
- **Josh Thomas** recognized as the **2022 UIL Class 5A Boys 160-pound State Wrestling Champion** — the first state champion in Caney Creek wrestling program history
- Introduced by Principal Dr. Jeff Stickler, Athletic Director Kendall Heinemann, and Head Coach Ferguson
- Thomas was a two-time district champion, regional champion, two-time state qualifier, and helped lead the team to two consecutive district team titles
- Coach Ferguson noted Thomas's work ethic, including purchasing his own mat during school shutdowns to continue training ("The Grangerland Regional Training Center")
- Board member quoted wrestler David Taylor III about wrestling being a lifestyle choice

### College Park High School — Three State Wrestling Champions
- Introduced by Principal Dr. Mark Murrell and Head Coach Eric Spuke (noted as UIL National High School Coaches Association Coach of the Year)
- Assistant coaches Lindsay Spute and Kenneth Mark also recognized

**Maddie Welch** (Senior, Girls Wrestling):
- State Champion; didn't start wrestling until sophomore year
- Did not qualify for state as a junior; dedicated herself to winning as a senior
- Also became a **National Champion** at the Women's High School National Championship in Colorado
- Has aspirations to wrestle in college

**Jacob Smith** (Senior, Boys Wrestling):
- State Champion; on the state podium three years (5th as freshman, 4th as junior, 1st as senior)
- 170 career wins — most in program history
- Will attend **Colorado School of Mines** on a wrestling scholarship, majoring in computer science

**Olivia De Giorgio** (Senior, Girls Wrestling):
- **Three-time State Champion** (sophomore, junior, senior) — fewer than a handful of wrestlers in Texas history have achieved this
- On the state podium all four years (5th as freshman)
- Moved up a weight class mid-season to help the team; defeated the defending state champion at the higher weight
- Finished 2nd at the Women's High School National Championship (lost by one point in finals)
- Will wrestle at **Texas Wesleyan University**

- The girls team finished **2nd place** at state (highest in program history); boys team finished **7th**

### Oak Ridge High School — UIL State Champion, Traditional Animation
- **Christina Lenz** (Senior) recognized as the **UIL Division II Traditional Animation State Champion**
- Introduced by Principal Dr. AJ Levesky and teacher Mr. Yale
- Finished 6th at state the previous year
- Created a three-minute stop-motion film called *Metamorphosis* using over 4,000 still images
- Mr. Yale noted this was an outside-of-class, no-grade project done over Christmas break
- Christina does not plan to pursue animation in college but excelled through the opportunity CISD provided
- Mr. Yale also previously had a South by Southwest champion in animation

### Special Life-Saving Recognition
- **Monica Jones**, teacher at Oak Ridge Elementary, shared her personal story:
  - On February 8, she went to bed ill and did not come to work the next day
  - Principal **Tammy Eldridge** became concerned when Jones didn't respond to texts/calls
  - Eldridge contacted **Officer Tamisha Wiley** (CISD Police) to check on Jones
  - Officer Wiley found Jones unresponsive; she and **Sergeant Michael Mann** called EMS
  - Jones was hospitalized with **sepsis and bacterial meningitis**; doctors told her she likely would not have survived a couple more hours
- **Tammy Eldridge**, **Officer Tamisha Wiley**, and **Sergeant Michael Mann** were recognized and honored for their actions
- Captain Blakelock spoke on behalf of the CISD Police Department
- Dr. Knoll referenced the Fred Rogers quote: "Look for the helpers"
- A brief recess was taken following this emotional recognition

---

## Citizen Participation (Item 3)

**Abby Colburn** — Senior, The Woodlands High School; co-president of Troop 1053 (theater department) and Texas Thespian State Officer:
- Advocated for theater education awareness, citing statistics (arts generate $6.1B in taxable sales in Texas; theater students twice as likely to graduate HS, 22% more likely to attend college, 15% more likely to get commended on standardized tests, 32% lower risk of depression)
- Presented a proclamation requesting March be designated "Theater at Our Schools Month" to bring awareness and funding to theater departments across CISD

**Diane Daniel:**
- Raised concerns about past mask mandates and the district's partnership with Kelsey-Seybold on COVID vaccinations
- Asked whether students who received COVID vaccines would be asked about it during physicals, given potential side effects
- Asked about the status of removing "sexual and pornographic books" from CISD libraries
- Praised the district's dyslexia programs and extracurricular activities

**Misty Oden-Weller:**
- Reiterated concerns from the prior month about teacher retention and unfilled positions
- Noted positions posted since August/September still unfilled (speech pathologists, special education teachers)
- Her grandson waited three months to see a speech pathologist
- Encouraged the board and Dr. Knoll to reach out informally to teachers to understand their needs and what is "wearing them down"
- Emphasized CISD's position as a premier district that should set the bar for attracting teachers

**Carolyn Nene:**
- Cited statistics about professional athletes suffering cardiac events post-COVID vaccination
- Referenced a Virginia pediatric physician requiring additional cardiac screening for vaccinated athletes before sports clearance
- Requested CISD add an addendum to the annual physical exam form asking if students received the COVID vaccine, and if so, requiring further diagnostic studies to rule out heart damage

**Jennifer Gulin:**
- Raised concerns about equitable allocation of funds for uniforms
- Conroe High School's mariachi program competed at state without traditional mariachi uniforms — the only school not in proper attire; students wore orchestra dress uniforms
- Drew an analogy to a marching band competing at state in concert attire
- Challenged the board to ensure equitable uniform funding in the budget process so Conroe's mariachi has proper uniforms next year

**Robert LaBelle:**
- Referenced Representative Toth's previous presentation about banning Critical Race Theory (CRT)
- Stated he heard some board members told Rep. Toth to "mind his own business" (noted he did not see the letters directly)
- Called for the district to push back against CRT, which he said is "infused in the subjects"

---

## Consent Agenda (Item 4)
- **Motion to approve** the consent agenda as presented
- **Vote: Passed unanimously** (all in favor by show of hands, none opposed)

---

## Item 12 (Moved Up) — Named Assistant Superintendent for High Schools
- Item moved up on the agenda with no objections
- Dr. Knoll noted Mr. Colson's retirement and praised his service
- Recommended **Dr. Chris Povich** as the new Assistant Superintendent for High Schools
  - Over 25 years in Conroe ISD
  - Served in multiple roles: teacher, assistant principal, associate principal, 9th grade principal, junior high principal, high school principal across multiple feeder zones
  - 8th position in the district
- **Vote: Passed unanimously** (all in favor by show of hands, none opposed)
- Dr. Povich thanked the board, Dr. Knoll, and Mr. Colson (his mentor for 13+ years)
- Introduced his family to the board
- Board members also praised Mr. Colson's contributions, including an anecdote about him jumping into the pool with the swim team at state championships

---

## Item 5A — Campus Mascot and School Colors for Gordon Reed Elementary
- Colors: **Navy blue, yellow, and gray**
- Students voted between Cubs and Gators; vote was nearly tied
- The district solicited feedback from **Ms. Annette Gordon Reed** herself
- Recommended mascot: **Titans**
- **Vote: Passed unanimously** (all in favor, none opposed)

---

## Item 6A — Adoption of Instructional Material for Health and Physical Education
- Dr. Upshaw presented the recommendation from the district instructional materials committee, reviewed through SHAC (Student Health Advisory Committee)
- Only resource meeting the state board list: **Goodheart-Willcox**
- Public posting had approximately 290 viewers since February 20 for feedback
- **Motion to approve**
- **Vote: Passed unanimously** (all in favor, none opposed)

### Additional Notes on Teaching & Learning
- Board members participated in Zoom meetings with principals and teachers the previous month; board expressed appreciation
- Board members also conducting Ambassador Award visits to campuses to recognize teachers

---

## Item 7A — Capital Improvements Update
Mr. Foster provided updates on multiple projects. **All projects reported as on schedule:**

| Project | Status/Notes |
|---|---|
| **Safety & Security 2022** | Camera/access control upgrades district-wide; on schedule through end of 2022 |
| **Gladys Elementary** | HVAC system overhaul; equipment procurement underway, work begins summer |
| **Creighton Elementary** | Kitchen expansion and utility upgrades; awaiting summer for completion |
| **New Moorhead Junior High** | Building taking shape; opens **August 2023** |
| **Conroe High School Renovation** | Construction going vertical — CTE building, athletic venues, JV/practice fields; phased turnovers starting mid-next school year through **December 2025** |
| **New JET Center** | **Substantial completion achieved** this month; receiving furniture |
| **Gordon Reed Elementary (Flex School #21)** | Interior finishes underway; opens **August 2022** |
| **Oak Ridge High School** | South County CTE facilities; new admin facility framing, new driveways; phase turnovers end of 2022, completion **August 2023** |
| **Conroe High School 9th Grade Addition** | Building in great shape; ready for students returning from summer break |
| **Flex School #22** (Grand Oaks HS feeder zone) | Site clearing underway; opens **August 2023** |
| **New Maintenance & Child Nutrition Facility** (FM 2854) | Demolition of existing building; opens **summer 2023** |
| **Caney Creek HS Upgrades** | Demolition underway for athletic facility expansion; completion **summer 2023** |

- **Bond Referendum Update (2019):** $321.3M expended to date; $330.1M remaining; $488.5M of approved $653.5M sold
- Discussion about the former West Tabernacle Baptist Church on the maintenance facility property — the church was able to purchase salvaged materials from the demolition contractor

---

## Item 8A — Award of RFP 21-1015: Self-Funded Health Plan ASO Medical Product Services
- Employee Benefits Committee (representatives from all feeder zones, auxiliary departments, and administration) recommended **Blue Cross Blue Shield (BCBS)**
- Five vendors submitted proposals: United Healthcare, Aetna, Cigna, BCBS, Healthcare Highways
- Two finalists (Aetna and BCBS) made final presentations
- BCBS recommended by **unanimous vote** of the Employee Benefits Committee
- **Estimated annual savings: approximately $8.2 million** compared to United Healthcare renewal rates
  - ~$7.7M from enhanced network efficiencies
  - ~$521K from reduced admin fees
  - BCBS put money up front to back savings guarantees
- Contract term: **2 years** beginning September 1, 2022, with three additional one-year renewal options
- Current insurer (United Healthcare) was in 3rd year; prior to that, Aetna for 20+ years
- Network disruption assessed as **very minimal** — main facilities near-identical match; Kelsey population exact match with enhancements
- BCBS/Memorial Hermann contract finalized earlier in March, resolving prior uncertainty
- Plan design and premium recommendations to come at the April work session; adoption vote at April board meeting
- Gallagher consultant Maggie Parker answered questions
- This is Gallagher's first BCBS school district client in the Houston area, though BCBS is the TPA for TRS statewide

**Vote: Passed unanimously** (all in favor, none opposed)

---

## Item 8B — Approval of ClassLink Inc. for Single Sign-On Suite
- Annual expenditure not to exceed **$160,000**
- ClassLink Suite replaces current SSO vendor (which was acquired by another company and removed required features including reverse proxy for home access)
- Features include two-factor authentication, analytics, modern UI, file access, user rostering
- Procured through interlocal agreement with Texas Department of Information Resources cooperative
- Funded from the general fund

**Vote: Passed unanimously** (all in favor, none opposed)

---

## Item 9 — Financial Statements (as of February 28, 2022)
Mrs. Garcia presented:
- **General Fund cash & investments:** $402.1 million total
  - Tax collections over 95% for the year
- **General Fund revenue YTD:** $478 million; **expenses:** $239.2 million
- **Debt Service:** Revenue $108.3M; expenses $81.1M
- **Self-Funded Insurance:** Running a deficit of ~$1.7M YTD (revenue $26.9M vs. expenses $28.6M), though January and February individually showed revenues exceeding expenditures
- **Investment portfolio par value:** $800.7 million
  - Combined yield: ~21 basis points, trailing slightly behind 90-day T-bill
  - Pool yields already rising to 35 basis points as of meeting date (expected to catch up within ~60 days following recent Fed rate increase)

---

## Closed Session
- Board entered closed session at **7:45 PM** under Texas Open Meetings Act sections 551.071 (consultation with attorney) and 551.074 (personnel matters)
- Board reconvened in open session at **8:52 PM**
- **No action reported** from closed session

---

## Adjournment
- Motion to adjourn; seconded and approved
- Meeting adjourned at approximately **8:52 PM**
